meaning: n.[动]水鹿,黑鹿(东南亚产的一种大鹿)
examples: Sambar is its milder cousin, made of lentils and vegetables, usually served with rice.
“Everyone gave money to the Liberation Tigers, ” says a 30-year-old waiter in Sambar, a Sri Lankan café in Harrow. “And we lost everything.”
The staple diet of most Indians consist of dal/sambar, rice, chapatis/rotis with cooked vegetables (mildly spiced/stir fried) or fish/meat.
